rationale: Review 1 would lead to the better PR because it identifies real naming problems visible in the diff: especially the contentful-looking `unipotent_radical_trivial`/`radical_trivial` names whose type is only `True`, the apostrophe wrapper `unipotentRadical_trivial'`, and the overly specific `AlgPoints` name. Its broad claim that all Prop classes need an `Is` prefix is overstated, but several findings are still material enough that an approve-only review would leave worse naming behind.
